claimText: Conservation Reserves are notified under Section 36A of the Wildlife Protection Act (WPA) 1972.
evidenceExcerpt: Conservation Reserves are declared under Section 36A of the Wildlife Protection Act (WPA) 1972.
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal backing of Conservation Reserves.

claimText: Conservation Reserves typically act as buffer zones or migration corridors between established national parks, sanctuaries, and forests.
evidenceExcerpt: They act as buffer zones or migration corridors between established national parks, sanctuaries, and forests.
examUse: Conceptual question on the ecological role of Conservation Reserves.

claimText: A Conservation Reserve Management Committee advises the Chief Wild Life Warden on the management of the reserve.
evidenceExcerpt: A Conservation Reserve Management Committee advises the Chief Wild Life Warden.
examUse: Statement-based question on the governance and administration of Conservation Reserves.
distractorHooks: Advises the National Board for Wildlife

claimText: Hesaraghatta Grassland is the last remaining grassland habitat in the Bengaluru region of Karnataka.
evidenceExcerpt: Hesaraghatta Grassland Location: Around Hesaraghatta Lake near Bengaluru in Karnataka and is the last remaining grassland habitat in Bengaluru region.
examUse: Location-based or correct-pair question matching grasslands to their respective states or regions.

claimText: Hesaraghatta Grassland forms a large catchment area for the Arkavathy River and Thippagondanahalli reservoir.
evidenceExcerpt: It forms a large catchment for the Arkavathy River and Thippagondanahalli reservoir.
examUse: Geography and Environment linkage question matching protected areas to their associated river systems.
